name: Coco Loco Brulee
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 325°F.
2. Place a 13x9-inch glass baking dish in a shallow roasting pan.
3. In a heavy saucepan, combine cream, coconut milk, cream of coconut, and vanilla bean. Bring to a boil over medium heat; remove from heat.
4. In a large bowl, whisk together half the sugar and egg yolks until smooth. Slowly whisk in hot cream mixture. Strain through a fine sieve into the baking dish. Pour 1/2 inch hot water into the roasting pan to create a water bath.
5. Place the roasting pan with the baking dish in the oven. Bake for 25-35 minutes, or until just barely set but still quivery. Do not overbake. Remove from the water bath and let cool to room temperature. Refrigerate until cold (custard can be made up to 1 day ahead; cover and refrigerate).
6. Crack coconuts in half with a hammer; reserve coconut water, if desired.
7. Just before serving, spoon custard into coconut halves. Smooth the top of the mixture with the back of a spoon. Sprinkle a tablespoon of the remaining sugar in a thin layer over the filling in each coconut.
8. With a blowtorch, caramelize the sugar.
9. Serve in shallow bowls.
Mini blowtorches are used by pastry chefs and cooks. They can be found in department stores and gourmet kitchen shops. A regular blowtorch also can be used.
